title: An Act to Prohibit Ownership of 6 Percent or More or Control of a Public Utility by an Entity Providing Aid to a Foreign Terrorist Organization
last_house_action: 5/22/2025 - Reports READ . Representative SACHS of Freeport, MOVED to ACCEPT the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report. Representative CARLOW of Buxton REQUESTED a division on the motion to ACCEPT the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report. The Chair ordered a division. A vote of the House was taken. 97 voted in favor of the same and 18 against, and accordingly the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report was ACCEPTED . Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
last_senate_action: 5/27/2025 - Reports Read On motion by Senator LAWRENCE of York the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report ACCEPTED in concurrence. Placed in Legislative Files (DEAD)

actions.description: Committee on Energy, Utilities and Technology suggested and ordered printed. The Bill was REFERRED to the Committee on ENERGY, UTILITIES AND TECHNOLOGY . Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
The Bill was REFERRED to the Committee on ENERGY, UTILITIES AND TECHNOLOGY in concurrence
Carried over, in the same posture, to the next special or regular session of the 132nd Legislature, pursuant to Joint Order SP 519.

Reports READ . Representative SACHS of Freeport, MOVED to ACCEPT the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report. Representative CARLOW of Buxton REQUESTED a division on the motion to ACCEPT the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report. The Chair ordered a division. A vote of the House was taken. 97 voted in favor of the same and 18 against, and accordingly the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report was ACCEPTED . Sent for concurrence. ORDERED SENT FORTHWITH.
Reports Read On motion by Senator LAWRENCE of York the Majority Ought Not to Pass Report ACCEPTED in concurrence. Placed in Legislative Files (DEAD)
